source_record.title: Multiple Myeloma Insights from Single-Cell Analysis: Clonal Evolution, the Microenvironment, Therapy Evasion, and Clinical Implications.
source_record.study_context: Cancers review article summarizing single-cell analysis in multiple myeloma, including clonal evolution, microenvironment interactions, therapy evasion, and clinical-translation challenges.

extracted_signals.signal_summary: The review describes multiple myeloma as heterogeneous and shaped by clonal evolution, genetic instability, and subclonal diversity across progression and treatment resistance.
limitations: Review-level signal, not a source-cohort measurement.
Does not quantify subclone frequency or relapse risk.

extracted_signals.signal_summary: The review frames the bone marrow tumor microenvironment as a supportive and immune-evasion context that single-cell technologies can help resolve.
limitations: Review-level category rather than a specific cohort extraction.
Does not identify a patient-specific microenvironment mechanism.
Does not establish clinical actionability of any microenvironment signal.

extracted_signals.signal_summary: The review notes that advanced single-cell technologies have clinical promise but face barriers including high costs, data complexity, standardization needs, and ethical considerations.
limitations: This is an implementation-readiness signal, not a biological mechanism.
Does not claim single-cell profiling is routine clinical decision support.
Does not recommend any test for an individual patient.

what_this_supports: The geometry pilot should preserve heterogeneity, microenvironment, and technology-readiness layers as separate map dimensions.
Single-cell review context can guide extraction fields for later source-specific primary-study records.
The map should keep biological plausibility separate from clinical implementation readiness.
what_this_does_not_support: It does not provide treatment guidance, diagnostic guidance, trial recommendations, or patient-specific interpretation.
It does not quantify mechanism frequency.
It does not establish that single-cell analysis is clinically ready for routine decision support.
It does not rank clonal, microenvironmental, or technical barriers by priority.

review_notes: Built from PubMed metadata and abstract-level review statements for PMID 40002248.
Review-level signals are separated from cohort-level observations.
All extracted signals are research-map signals and are not clinical advice.
